what_it_does: Implements a process node that converts a single Text input into a Vector by calling an OpenAI-compatible embeddings endpoint through fabric.client.complete_embeddings, with model, endpoint URL, retries, and expected vector dimension driven by CONFIG defaults.
observations: PORTS_IN/PORTS_OUT declare a Text input and a Vector output.
CONFIG uses nested field descriptors with type, label, default, and an options_from registry reference for the model.
Default URL, model, and dimension are imported from fabric.config rather than hardcoded in the node.
run() instantiates transport.openai_embeddings_transport and calls client.complete_embeddings on a one-element list, returning the first vector.
Expected dimension is passed into the client call and enforced there.
Imports from fabric are deferred inside run(), reducing import-time side effects during node discovery.
standouts: Vector is introduced as a first-class port type distinct from Text.
Model options are resolved from a live registry (embed_models) rather than a static enum list.
The expected dimension is treated as an explicit, enforced config slot rather than an implicit constant.
conventions: Declares VERSION, KIND, PORTS_IN, PORTS_OUT, CONFIG, and run() per the process-node contract.
Config defaults delegate to fabric.config constants to avoid literal duplication.
Uses lazy imports inside run() to keep node discovery lightweight.
Uses defensive .get() fallbacks in run() so a bare config still executes.
concerns: run() does no explicit validation that inputs['text'] exists or is a string beyond str().
retries and dim are not validated against CONFIG min/max inside run(); enforcement is assumed external.
No visible error handling for network or embedding failures in this file.
The comment mentions batching semantics but run() always wraps the input in a single-element list, so batching is not exposed.
notes: A config-driven embedding leaf node that delegates endpoint details to fabric.config and the fabric client, while exposing model and dimension as inspector-tunable fields.
questions: What is the exact schema returned by embed_models and how is options_from resolved at runtime?
How does complete_embeddings enforce dim and what exception type does it raise on mismatch?
Is transport.openai_embeddings_transport reusable/cached, or created per call?
What does the inspector do if the registry at embed_models is unreachable?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches actual: it is a pure text-to-vector process node that calls the embeddings fabric with config-driven endpoint and enforced dimension.
apparent_themes: Config-driven node descriptors over hardcoded values.
Registry-sourced model lists instead of hand-typed enums.
Dimension contracts enforced loudly rather than silently corrupting downstream comparisons.
Lazy runtime imports to keep node discovery lightweight.
intention_signals: The file is trying to make embedding a first-class, inspectable, swappable node in a fabric graph, where endpoint, model, and dimension are externally configurable but correctness (especially vector dimension) is enforced by the underlying client.
novelty: Treats Vector as a distinct port type and pushes model enumeration to a live registry rather than a static config list; also makes expected dimension an explicit, enforced config slot.
contribution: Provides a reusable text-to-vector process node with a declared type contract, config-driven endpoint/model/dimension, and integration with the fabric embeddings client.
summary_for_embedding: company/nodes/embed.py defines a process node that embeds a Text input into a Vector using a configurable OpenAI-compatible embeddings endpoint and enforces expected vector dimension via fabric.client.complete_embeddings.
intention_for_embedding: To offer a graph-compatible, inspector-configurable embedding leaf node that keeps endpoint and model choices in config while guaranteeing dimension correctness.

symbols.does: Executes the embedding call for a single text input and returns the resulting vector.
symbols.description: Resolves model, endpoint, retries, and expected dimension from config (with module defaults as fallbacks), opens an OpenAI-compatible embeddings transport, and invokes fabric.client.complete_embeddings on a one-element list, returning the first vector. It is the runtime implementation of the embed process node.
